title: The Lifecycle of an Agent Run
concept: Tracing a task from initial dispatch, routing, planning, loop execution, evaluation, to database persistence.

exercises.prompt: What is the correct sequence of the perceive-reason-act loop in agentic AI?
exercises.explain: The perceive-reason-act loop starts with 'Perceive' to gather information, followed by 'Reasoning' to make decisions, and then 'Act' to perform actions.

cards.title: What is an Agent Run?
cards.body: An agent run is the complete journey of an AI agent performing a task, from the moment it receives the task to completing it and storing the results.

cards.title: Lifecycle of an Agent Run
cards.body: This lifecycle includes six main stages: dispatch, routing, planning, loop execution, evaluation, and database persistence.

cards.title: The Agent Run Journey
cards.body: Think of it like a train journey. It starts at a station (dispatch), moves along track choices (routing), stops at several stations to pick up and drop off (planning and executing), checks if it reached the final destination (evaluation), and logs its journey in a travel log (database persistence).

cards.title: Example: AI Assistant
cards.body: When you ask an AI assistant to remind you of something, it follows the lifecycle: it gets the request (dispatch), figures out where to set the reminder (routing), plans when and how to remind you (planning), executes the reminder (loop execution), checks if the reminder was delivered successfully (evaluation), and saves it in memory (database persistence).

cards.title: Myth: AI Agents Act Alone
cards.body: Some people think AI agents operate without guidance; however, they always follow structured processes to complete tasks successfully.

cards.title: Visualizing the Lifecycle
cards.body: Understanding how each stage connects helps clarify agent runs. Think of it like a map of an adventure.

exercises.prompt: What happens after an AI agent receives a task?

options.text: It routes the task for planning and execution.
It ignores the task until prompted again.
It runs the task without any planning.

exercises.explain: After receiving a task, the AI agent routes it for planning and execution to determine the best way to handle it.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The evaluation stage happens before loop execution in an agent run.
exercises.explain: Evaluation comes after loop execution to ensure the task was completed successfully.

exercises.prompt: An agent's journey includes stages such as dispatch, routing, planning, loop execution, evaluation, and ______.
exercises.sentence: Finally, the agent stores the completed task in the database for future reference.
exercises.explain: The correct term is 'database persistence', describing how the agent saves its results.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following describes the planning stage best?

options.text: The agent decides how to act based on the task.
The agent finishes the task without thinking.
The agent only evaluates outcomes at this stage.

exercises.explain: The planning stage is where the agent decides on actions based on how to best tackle the task.

exercises.prompt: Which stages are necessary for a complete agent run?

options.text: Dispatch, Routing, Planning, Execution, Evaluation, Database Persistence

exercises.explain: A complete agent run goes through all six stages: dispatching the task, routing it, planning actions, executing those actions, evaluating results, and finally persisting data in the database.
